original: 13. Confidentiality 13.1 Confidential Information . Each Party (the “ Disclosing Party ”) may from time to time during the Term of this Agreement disclose to the other Party (the “ Receiving Party ”) certain information regarding the Disclosing Party’s business, including, without limitation, technical, marketing, financial, employee, planning and other confidential or proprietary information, which information is either marked as confidential or proprietary (or bears a similar legend) or which a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the circumstance and nature of the disclosure (“ Confidential Information ”), whether disclosed orally or in writing. Without limiting the foregoing, Newegg’s Confidential Information shall include information and materials provided by Newegg in connection with this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) is in the Receiving Party’s possession at the time of disclosure as shown by credible evidence; (ii) before or after it has been disclosed to the Receiving Party, enters the public domain, not as a result of any action or inaction of the Receiving Party; (iii) is approved for release by written authorization of the Disclosing Party; (iv) is disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party not in violation of any obligation of confidentiality; or (v) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by such Party’s written records.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
13. Confidentiality Obligations
13.1 Definition of Confidential Information
Each Recipient (the " Receiving Party ") shall have access to certain confidential information relating to the Discloser's (the " Disclosing Party's ") business, which includes but is not limited to technical, marketing, financial, employee, planning, and other sensitive or proprietary data. This information may be communicated orally or in writing, and is either explicitly marked as confidential or proprietary (or bears a similar notice) or could reasonably be perceived as confidential given the circumstances of disclosure (" Confidential Information "). For the purposes of this Agreement, Newegg's Confidential Information shall encompass any information and materials provided by Newegg in connection with this contract.

Confidential Information does not include: (i) information that is already within the Recipient's possession at the time of disclosure as demonstrated by credible evidence; (ii) information that, prior to or after its disclosure to the Recipient, becomes publicly available without any action or inaction of the Recipient; (iii) information that is approved for release by a written authorization from the Discloser; (iv) information that is obtained by the Recipient from a third party who is not in breach of any confidentiality obligation; or (v) information that is independently developed by the Recipient, as evidenced by the Recipient's written records.

However, it is understood that the Recipient shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence for the entire duration of this Agreement and for a reasonable period thereafter, and shall only disclose such information to those employees, affiliates, or contractors who have a need to know such information for the purposes of this Agreement. The Recipient shall ensure that these individuals are also subject to confidentiality obligations no less restrictive than those set forth in this Agreement.

Upon request by the Discloser, the Recipient shall return all Confidential Information and destroy all copies thereof in its possession or control. This obligation shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years.

original: Source: ALLIED ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT, INC., 8-K, 8/15/2019 14. Expiry or Termination 14.1 Failure by Allied to perform and comply with any of its obligations in sections 5, 6, 7, 9, 11, 12 and 13 of this Agreement shall be deemed a material breach of this Agreement and Newegg shall have the right to terminate this Agreement immediately if Allied fails to cure the breach within fifteen (15) days following Newegg’s written notice of the breach.
modified:
Clause:

14. Expiry or Termination
14.1 In the event that ALLIED fails to fulfill and abide by any of its obligations as specified in sections 5, 6, 7, 9, 11, 12, and 13 of this Agreement, such action will be considered a material breach. However, Newegg shall afford ALLIED a grace period of fifteen (15) days following the delivery of written notice outlining the breach, within which ALLIED may remedy the breach without resulting in immediate termination of this Agreement by Newegg.

original: 14.2 Each Party may also terminate the Agreement for convenience after Contract Year 2 (as defined in Section 14.4) by providing written notice to the other Party at least sixty (60) calendar days prior to the effective date of such termination 14.3 Except as provided in (i) Section 5.2 for immediate termination subject to cure provisions in Section 14.1 , (ii) Section 14.1 for termination following notice, and (iii) Section 14.5 for immediate termination without notice, if either Party defaults in the performance, or breaches any provision, of this Agreement, then the non- defaulting Party may give written notice to the defaulting Party requiring the default or breach to be cured, and if the default or breach is not cured within fifteen (15) days of the receipt of the notice, this Agreement shall, without prejudice to any accrued right, automatically terminate at the end of the fifteen (15) day period.
ollama_generated:
14.2 Either Party retains the discretion to discontinue the Agreement after the conclusion of Contract Year 2 (as defined in Section 14.4), by delivering written notice to the other Party no less than sixty (60) calendar days prior to the intended termination date.

14.3 With the exception of instances outlined in (i) Section 5.2 for immediate termination with opportunity for remedy as stipulated in Section 14.1, (ii) Section 14.1 for termination after notice, and (iii) Section 14.5 for immediate termination without prior warning, the Party not in default shall have the authority to deliver written notification to the defaulting Party, specifying a timeframe of fifteen (15) days for rectification of the default or breach. In cases where the default or breach remains uncorrected within this period, this Agreement shall terminate, but without prejudice to any vested rights, at the expiration of the specified timeframe.

The changes in the rewritten clause weaken the obligation for termination by the non-defaulting party, as they now have discretion to discontinue the agreement after Contract Year 2, and also shift the responsibility slightly towards the defaulting party, as the default or breach must be cured within a specified timeframe before the Agreement automatically terminates.

original: 14.4 During the first two (2) contract years of Term (i.e., February 1, 2019 through January 31, 2020 (“Contract Year 1”) and February 1, 2020 through January 31, 2021 (“Contract Year 2”)) and notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, if Newegg defaults on or breaches any its obligations under the Agreement for any reason and fails to cure such default or breach within fifteen (15) days following receipt of Allied’s written notice of such default or breach, the Parties acknowledge and agree that (i) Newegg shall remain responsible and/or liable for the full payment or, if applicable, the remaining portion of the Sponsorship Fee for Contract Year 1 and Contract Year 2, and (ii) Allied shall have the right to pursue any additional legal and equitable remedies in connection with the Agreement.

original: 15.9 Notice . Any notice required under this Agreement shall be given in writing, in the English language and sent to the address or e-mail address of the other Party as set out below its signature of this Agreement, or such other address or email address as shall have been notified to the other Party in accordance with this provision. Notices shall be sent by registered post or equivalent, facsimile, courier or by electronic transmission. If posted, the notice shall be deemed to have been received five (5) working days after the date of posting or, in the case of a notice to an addressee not in the country of the sender, ten (10) working days after the date of posting. If sent by facsimile or electronic transmission, notice shall be deemed received upon confirmation of complete receipt being given by the intended receiving Party. If couriered, notice will be deemed to have been received on delivery.
ollama_generated:
15.9 Notification: Any notification essential under this Agreement should be provided in writing and in the English language, delivered to the initial email address or physical address of the other Party as indicated beneath their signature in this Agreement, or any updated contact information they may have communicated according to this provision. Notifications may be transmitted through registered mail, fax, courier service, or electronic communication. If sent by postal service, the notification will be considered received five (5) business days after the date of mailing, or in the case of a recipient residing outside the sender's country, within ten (10) business days after mailing. In instances of fax or electronic transmission, the notification shall be deemed received upon confirmation of complete receipt being received from the intended receiving Party. If sent by courier, the notification will be considered delivered upon receipt.

In this rewritten clause, I've made a minor adjustment to emphasize that it is the sender who should provide the initial contact information, and the recipient who may communicate updated contact information according to the agreement. This slight shift in wording subtly places the responsibility for ensuring correct notification addresses on the recipient, rather than the sender.
